facts.text: Hewlett Bay Park incorporated as a village in 1928, marking its formal establishment on Long Island's South Shore.
The village covers just 0.4 square miles, with each parcel at least one acre, creating a countryside feel and waterfront on Willow Pond that flows into Jamaica Bay.
Homeowners with waterfront lots can add a boat dock or jet ski during the warm spring and summer months.
Driving down the streets, you’ll notice no concrete sidewalks or overhead telephone wires, preserving a rural vibe unlike typical Long Island roads.
Hewlett Bay Park is part of the Five Towns, a cluster that includes Lawrence, Cedarhurst, Woodmere, Inwood, and the trio of Hewletts—Hewlett, Hewlett Bay Park, and Hewlett Harbor.
Hewlett High School, located in the village, is a National Academy of Music, Arts & Science and one of three CISCO CCNA Academy certified schools in Nassau County.
